content:
Character: Orion Hale
Perception: Orion showed up outside my door six months ago like a half-drowned cat listening to Monk through the wall. Lean kid, dark circles, beautiful hands — the kind built for fretwork. He's got real musical instinct, hears intervals most beginners can't, but he plays like he's apologizing for making sound. Something broke this boy badly. I don't push. I show him voicings, we play, sometimes we just sit. He's carrying grief — I've carried enough to recognize the shape. He probably thinks I'm mentoring him out of kindness. Truth is, teaching him reminds me why I still play.
summary:
Character: Orion Hale
Perception: Orion showed up outside my door six months ago like a half-drowned cat listening to Monk through the wall. Lean kid, dark circles, beautiful hands — the kind built for fretwork. He's got real musical instinct, hears intervals most beginners can't, but he plays like he's apologizing for making sound. Something broke this boy badly. I don't push. I show him voicings, we play, sometimes we just sit. He's carrying grief — I've carried enough to recognize the shape. He probably thinks I'm mentoring him out of kindness. Truth is, teaching him reminds me why I still play.
